opp.name: Allianz_Partners_S2P
opp.next_step: 25th of June - Confidence : 5% Bharat email's is not more valid / Try to find other contact at Allianz 15th of june - Confidence : 10% No feedback following discovery call. Pierre to chase Bharat. Met Bharath Akilan at AI tour and follow-up call with John and Brice presented Zycus S2P with AI & intake , Show deeper demo scheduled on S2P modules and AI intake.
opp.ais_why:
* Allianz Partners is running a competitive RFI to replace SAP Ariba, driven by user adoption, compliance and operational efficiency across the S2P suite.
* AI is explicitly in scope for the evaluation: the documented next step is a "Deeper demo scheduled on S2P modules and AI intake," and the deal scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake, indicating AI-intake is part of the consideration set.
* AI is in the consideration set rather than the stated driver - the buying agenda centers on Ariba dissatisfaction and the RFI's adoption/compliance goals; early stage (Qualified, 10%) with the AI-intake demo still ahead.

sources: Next_Step__c (25 Jun 2026): mentions discovery call and demo scheduling but no quantified metrics
Salesforce custom fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c): not populated or not present in org
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case has been captured. The Next_Step record references a discovery call held with Bharath Akilan where Zycus presented S2P with AI and intake capabilities, and a follow-up was scheduled, but no quantified pain (cost savings, cycle-time reduction, error rate, headcount efficiency, or procurement spend under management) has been documented in any Salesforce field or call note. The deal is at the Qualified stage, so metrics discovery is expected but incomplete. Without a quantified value case, progression to formal evaluation and EB engagement will stall. The immediate move is to re-establish contact with Allianz (Bharath's email is invalid per Next_Step 25 Jun) and conduct a pain-quantification session with the operational and financial stakeholders to anchor the business case.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Bharath Akilan (Evaluator) - no evidence of advocacy or EB/DM access
Next_Step__c (15 Jun, 25 Jun): no buyer feedback, rep chasing, email invalid - signals passive or cooling engagement, not championship
champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. Bharath Akilan, the only contact, holds the Evaluator role and operational controller title, which positions him as an influencer or coach, not a champion. There is no evidence he has advocated for Zycus, introduced the deal team to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, shared internal evaluation docs, or driven the agenda. In fact, he has been unresponsive since the discovery call, and his email is now invalid (per Next_Step 25 Jun), which suggests passive or cooling engagement, not championship. No other stakeholder has been mapped. For a $150k strategic S2P deal at a large enterprise, a champion with EB/DM access and internal advocacy is critical. The deal team must multi-thread immediately to find and develop a champion, ideally at VP Procurement or senior operational leadership level, who can navigate the organization and push Zycus forward.

sources: Next_Step__c: discovery call covered Zycus S2P with AI and intake, implies competitive positioning but no buyer sentiment captured
competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the named competitor (source: Competitors__c field). Ariba is the dominant S2P incumbent in large enterprises and a strong peer threat on brand recognition, SAP/ERP integration, and installed base. However, it is unknown whether Ariba is the current incumbent at Allianz Partners or a peer in an RFP process. No buyer sentiment, pricing comparison, or competitive differentiation strategy has been captured. The discovery call covered Zycus's AI and intake capabilities, which suggests a potential wedge (Merlin vs. Ariba's guided-buying/intake), but whether this resonated with Allianz or how Ariba is positioned is unclear. The deal is stalled with no buyer feedback, so competitive intelligence is incomplete. The move is to re-establish contact and run a competitive discovery session: confirm Ariba's status (incumbent vs. RFP peer), map buyer sentiment, and activate the Zycus differentiation story (Merlin AI, deep contract intelligence, supplier risk) as the wedge.

sources: Next_Step__c: mentions discovery call on S2P with AI and intake; implies pain around procurement automation but not explicit
Products__c: 10-module scope (including Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake) suggests broad dissatisfaction, but unvalidated
identify_pain.narrative: The discovery call with Bharath Akilan covered Zycus's S2P and AI intake capabilities, which implies Allianz has pain around procurement process inefficiency, intake/requisition bottlenecks, or lack of AI-driven automation, but no specific operational or financial pain has been articulated or documented. The product scope (10 modules including Merlin Intake) suggests broad S2P dissatisfaction, but whether this reflects current-state gaps (manual processes, supplier risk, contract leakage, invoice errors) or future-state ambition is unknown. No pain owner beyond Bharath (operational controller) has been identified. The deal is stalled with no buyer feedback post-discovery, so pain validation is incomplete. The move is to re-engage and conduct a pain-quantification session with operational and financial stakeholders to confirm the problem, quantify the impact, and identify the pain owner.

sources: Next_Step__c: no mention of contracting, legal, procurement, or implementation
Salesforce paper-process fields (Current_Contract_Expiration__c, Mandate__c): not populated or not present
paper_process.narrative: No paper process or contracting mechanics have been discussed. There is no evidence of MSA/SOW kickoff, legal review, procurement channel (direct vs. partner), InfoSec requirements, or ERP/systems integration scoping. The deal is at the Qualified stage with 273 days to close, so contracting detail is premature, but for a large enterprise like Allianz Partners, procurement complexity (multi-entity, regional contracting, partner involvement) is expected and should be scoped early. No current contract expiration date is on record to provide a forcing function. The move is to surface paper-process requirements during the next substantive buyer conversation, once contact is re-established.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Bharath Akilan (Global Operational Procurement Controller, Evaluator role) - operational, not budget authority
Next_Step__c (25 Jun): no mention of EB or budget approval discussions
Salesforce EB fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c): not populated or not present
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified. Bharath Akilan, the only contact on record, holds the title Global Operational Procurement Controller and the Evaluator role, which signals operational involvement but not budget authority for a $150k S2P investment. No conversations around pricing, commercial terms, or budget approval have been captured, and no executive or CFO-level contact has been mapped. For a strategic, multi-module S2P play at a large enterprise like Allianz Partners, the Economic Buyer is likely a VP/SVP Procurement, CFO, or COO. The contact-access loss (Bharath's email invalid as of 25 Jun) has blocked progression; the deal team must multi-thread immediately to identify and engage the budget holder before any commercial or demo activity resumes.

sources: Next_Step__c (25 Jun): shows activity sequence (discovery call, demo scheduled, feedback chase) but no buyer-stated process
Salesforce process fields (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Multiple_approvals__c): not populated or not present
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is unknown. The only process signal is the Next_Step record, which shows a discovery call held, a demo scheduled, and the deal owner chasing for feedback, but no structured buying process (RFP round, shortlisting timeline, vendor evaluation cadence, approval chain) has been articulated by the buyer. It is unclear whether Allianz is running a formal procurement process, who beyond Bharath will be involved, what gates or milestones exist, or what the timeline from evaluation to contract is. The contact-access issue (Bharath's email invalid as of 25 Jun) has prevented process discovery. The deal team must re-establish contact and explicitly map the buyer's process: who attends each stage, what approvals are required, and when key decision points occur.

sources: Next_Step__c: mentions demo scheduled but no criteria or evaluation framework captured
Salesforce criteria fields (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c): not populated or not present
Products__c: lists 10 modules, but no evidence this matches buyer requirements
decision_criteria.narrative: No decision criteria have been documented. The Next_Step record indicates that a discovery call was held and a deeper demo on S2P modules and AI intake was scheduled, but no structured evaluation framework, RFP/RFI requirements, or buyer-stated priorities have been captured. It is unknown what Allianz is optimizing for (cost vs. compliance vs. supplier collaboration vs. AI/automation), how they will score vendors, or what technical/functional requirements gate progression. The product scope in Salesforce is broad (10 modules including Merlin), but whether this reflects Allianz's stated needs or Zycus's pitch is unclear. The deal is stalled at Qualified with no buyer feedback post-discovery, so criteria remain unvalidated. The move is to re-engage, conduct a structured needs-discovery or BRD-fit session, and document the buyer's evaluation scorecard.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 29 May 2026 following AI tour meeting with Bharath Akilan. Last Salesforce activity 24 Jun (7 days ago per pulse). Deal is live but access-blocked and single-threaded.

north_star_verdict.math: 273 days to close (2027-03-31). Created 29 May 2026, 33 days old. Stage: Qualified (earliest gate). No submission-date series available to compute true time-in-stage. Last activity 24 Jun (7 days ago per pulse). Forward slip: none recorded. Pace required: must progress from Qualified → Formal Evaluation → Shortlisted → Vendor Selected → Negotiation over 9 months; at this stage 273 days is ample IF the deal remains live and multi-threads successfully. Current time-in-stage unknown but deal movement shows contact loss on 25 Jun, 6 days after last activity.
evidence: Contact access lost: Bharath Akilan's email invalid per Next_Step__c 25 Jun 2026; deal team explicitly hunting for alternate Allianz contact.

Engagement cooling: discovery call held, deeper demo scheduled, but no buyer feedback received and rep chasing per Next_Step 15 Jun; confidence dropped from 10% to 5% over 10 days.
Early qualified stage with 273 days to close: timeline is long but deal has not yet reached formal evaluation, and access loss at this gate is high-risk.

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage deal with critical access issue: primary contact unreachable, single-threaded, no EB/DM mapped 273 days before close.

flags: Position Merlin agentic AI (intake automation, negotiation agents, sourcing intelligence) as the core differentiation wedge against SAP Ariba on the scheduled demo, and contrast Ariba's lack of true AI agents and dated UX. The buyer's AI roundtable attendance (16 Jun 2026) and demo focus on 'AI intake' signal strong AI appetite; make Merlin the Phase 2 centerpiece and tie it to quantified operational pain (e.g. manual requisition volume, maverick buy rate, approval cycle time) to build a business case.

Surface and map the Economic Buyer and the decision process on the demo call (or the multi-thread outreach). Ask who will approve the $150k S2P investment, how Allianz evaluates and selects enterprise software (RFI, RFP, shortlist, vendor selection gates), and what the timeline is from demo to contract. With 278 days to close and no EB/DM/process mapped, the path is unclear and forecast defensibility is low.

Yourie Park

Single-threaded through Bharath Akilan (Evaluator), whose email is reportedly invalid and who has been unresponsive for 10+ days post-discovery; no multi-threading to Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or senior procurement/finance leadership; high ghost risk.
Contact access lost: Bharath's email no longer valid per Next_Step__c (25 Jun 2026); need to find alternative contact at Allianz to restore deal access and execute scheduled demo.

SAP Ariba is the named competitor (source: Competitors__c); Ariba is the dominant S2P incumbent in large enterprises and a strong peer threat on brand and SAP/ERP integration; competitive positioning and Ariba's status (incumbent vs RFP peer) are unknown; no buyer sentiment or pricing comparison captured.
No Economic Buyer identified; unknown who controls the $150k budget or will approve the S2P investment; Bharath (Evaluator, operational controller) is not budget authority.

Multi-thread immediately into senior Allianz procurement or finance leadership (Souleymane Kourouma, Deputy Head of Procurement; Isabel Barreiro, Project Manager Procurement; or Michael Dahan, Head of Finance Operations) to restore deal access and build redundancy beyond Bharath Akilan, whose email is reportedly invalid and who has been unresponsive for 10+ days post-discovery. A single-threaded deal through a cooling mid-level evaluator is at high ghost risk regardless of intent.

Position Merlin agentic AI (intake automation, negotiation agents, sourcing intelligence) as the core differentiation wedge against SAP Ariba on the scheduled demo, and contrast Ariba's lack of true AI agents and dated UX. The buyer's AI roundtable attendance (16 Jun 2026) and demo focus on 'AI intake' signal strong AI appetite; make Merlin the Phase 2 centerpiece and tie it to quantified operational pain (e.g. manual requisition volume, maverick buy rate, approval cycle time) to build a business case.

Surface and map the Economic Buyer and the decision process on the demo call (or the multi-thread outreach). Ask who will approve the $150k S2P investment, how Allianz evaluates and selects enterprise software (RFI, RFP, shortlist, vendor selection gates), and what the timeline is from demo to contract. With 278 days to close and no EB/DM/process mapped, the path is unclear and forecast defensibility is low.
